title: Nursery Rhyme. CCXXIII. Riddles.
content:
[One leg is a leg of mutton; two legs, a man; three legs, a stool; four legs, a dog.]

        Two legs sat upon three legs,
        With one leg in his lap;
        In comes four legs,
        And runs away with one leg.
        Up jumps two legs,
        Catches up three legs,
        Throws it after four legs,
        And makes him bring back one leg.
meaning:
"Nursery Rhyme. CCXXIII. Riddles." is a quintessential example of Unknown's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
